         .           /**
         .            * @brief Draw a uniform [0, 1) random number from an epi_xoshiro256ss engine.
         .            *
         .            * Uses the top N bits of the 64-bit output (where N =
         .            * std::numeric_limits<epiworld_double>::digits) to produce a value in [0, 1)
         .            * without any long-double arithmetic.
         .            *
         .            * @param engine An epi_xoshiro256ss engine.
         .            * @return epiworld_double in [0, 1).
         .            */
   600,830 ( 0.00%)  inline epiworld_double runif_epi(epi_xoshiro256ss & engine) {
   120,166 ( 0.00%)      constexpr int bits  = std::numeric_limits<epiworld_double>::digits;
   120,166 ( 0.00%)      constexpr int shift = 64 - bits;
         .               // scale = 2^{-bits}: the result is in [0, 1) by construction (no clamp needed)
   240,332 ( 0.00%)      constexpr epiworld_double scale =
         .                   epiworld_double(1) / epiworld_double(uint64_t(1) << bits);
 1,321,826 ( 0.01%)      return static_cast<epiworld_double>(engine() >> shift) * scale;
10,814,940 ( 0.05%)  => tests/../include/epiworld/rng-utils.hpp:epiworld::epi_xoshiro256ss::operator()() (120,166x)
   240,332 ( 0.00%)  }
